title: Canistropsis marceloi
taxonomy: Canistropsis marceloi is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is native to Brazil and is part of the genus Canistropsis, which consists of several species of epiphytic plants. The classification of Canistropsis marceloi places it within the order Poales, which is a group of monocots that includes various types of grasses and flowering plants. This classification also groups it with other members of the family Bromeliaceae, such as pineapples and air plants. Further classification at the rank of family, order, and class places Canistropsis marceloi in the superorder Commelinids.

intro: Canistropsis marceloi, commonly known as the Marcelo's bromeliad, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, boasting various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Canistropsis marceloi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
